rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO** CONSERVATORSHIP OF NORMA SHIRAISHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Transfer Of Property Belonging To The Conservatorship Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/04/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Notice of the hearing (filed 02/26/2020) does not contain the information required by Probate Code section 851(c)(1) & (3). Amended notice must be served. 2) The proof of service (filed 02/26/2020) does not show service of process on Old Republic Title Company. While Old Republic Title Company, through their attorney, filed a non-opposition acknowledging receipt of "notice" of the petition, it is unclear if they received a copy of the petition with the notice as required by Probate Code section 851(a) as a person having title to or possession of the disputed funds.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE KATIE HYDE 1999 LIVING U/T/D 7/22/19999 AS AMENDED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (582 40th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94121)
rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required. LRSF 14.3(A)(3) & CRC 7.452. The counsel is to provide updated proposed orders to the Court at the confirmation hearing. Prior to the hearing, the following issues must be addressed: 1) The legal description set forth in the report (Exhibit A) and proposed order is slightly different from the description set forth in the reappraisal ("Easterly 82 feet and 5 inches" or "Easterly 82 feet and 6 inches"). 2) If the settlor's "personal effects" as specified on Attachment 4f are proposed to be sold with the real property, the trustee's declaration addressing the application of CRC 7.1059(b)(17) must be filed. 3) The trustee's declaration confirming whether she has discussed the proposed sale with the beneficiary in compliance with Probate Code section 2540(b) does not appear to be on file. 4) The trustee's declaration explaining how the additional bond of $210,500 (item 6b of the report) was calculated must be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD LEWIS DERMEG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/13/2020 (no earlier date available due to court directive). The following issues must be addressed: 1) The legal description for the Madrid Street property set forth in Attachments 7a & 7b to the subject petition is slightly different from the description in the 07/12/2002 deed ("1/2 of Lot No. 14" or "Lot No. 14"). 2) The petitioner's supplemental declaration requests an order confirming one-half of community property belonging to her as the surviving spouse, but the quitclaim deeds (03/19/2015) indicate that the petitioner's interests in both properties have already been transferred to her trust. Please explain why the petitioner is entitled to the Court's confirmation of her interests held in the trust through this spousal property set-aside proceeding. 3) The 10/15/1985 deed for the Lisbon Street property indicates that the petitioner and decedent held title as joint tenants. Did the quitclaim deed operate as a unilateral severance of their joint tenancy pursuant to Civil Code section 683.2? If so, please explain why the petitioner's severed separate property interest can be confirmed by the Court through this spousal property set-aside proceeding. 4) Attachment 7a was not attached to the proposed order.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF CLAUDINE BECERRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: continued to 4/27/20 due to the current skeletal staff in the court. no later than 4/20/20, petitioner must file a supplement to the petition addressing the fact that he has an official record of the marriage and explaining why this petition is appropriate and necessary. please see also#5.a. to the declaration in support of marriage, wherein it states this procedure cannot establish the validity of a california marriage if no marriage license was obtained.
